lines: Hester Briarhook, thorncutter by trade and lantern-keeper by stubbornness.
Step under the hazel arch when the road narrows. I have trimmed it just enough for brave boots and honest elbows.
The arch will not bow for hurry, but it will make room for a courteous traveler.
Mind the upper boughs. They catch tall hats, loose tempers, and once an entire picnic basket.
